title: Prosecutors Have Moved Far Too Slowly in the Jeffrey Epstein Case
content:
The life and mysterious death of Jeffrey Epstein continue to fuel a cynical view that powerful elites are inherently corrupt. Epstein, a financier and convicted offender, died in a Manhattan prison cell in 2019 before facing full justice for trafficking and abusing vulnerable women and girls. Recently, under immense public pressure, the Department of Justice released over three million pages of related documents. Volunteer software engineers analyzed these massive files, scoring 1.4 million emails to reveal an astonishing network of influence and favor-trading. Nearly 60% of these emails involved people Epstein paid to handle bureaucratic complications or scrub his criminal record from the internet.

The remainder of the emails expose deep connections with prominent financiers, scientists, politicians, and academics. While some of these contacts may genuinely not have grasped the extent of his crimes, others appear deeply implicated or morally compromised. Unfortunately, the Department of Justice's haphazard redactions of these documents have sometimes shielded criminal abusers while inadvertently exposing the identities of innocent victims. This mishandling tragically echoes Epstein’s own abuses by reducing female victims to mere objects in the legal process. Ultimately, it remains baffling and reprehensible that prosecutors have made so little progress in bringing his enablers to justice over the past seven years.
